topic: A college student discovers their roommate has been reading their journal
raw_output:
[story 1]

The air in the room felt suddenly thick, suffocating. I slammed my textbook shut, the sound echoing sharply in the small space. My gaze snapped to the desk, and there it was: my journal, spine cracked open, pages filled with my most private ramblings, the ink starkly legible under the harsh desk lamp. A cold, hard knot tightened in my chest, radiating outward. I stood up, my movements jerky and sharp. "What are you doing?" My voice came out tight, strained. The roommate, engrossed in the book, looked up, a flicker of something unreadable crossing their face before they quickly looked away, a slight, dismissive shrug accompanying the movement. I walked over, my fists clenched so tightly my knuckles were white. "Don't touch that," I managed, the words low and vibrating with suppressed intensity. The silence that followed was deafening, filled only by the frantic drumming of my own pulse.

[story 2]

I found the evidence tucked beneath a pile of discarded laundry—my journal, somehow rearranged, the pages turned to reveal intimate details I hadn't intended for anyone to see. A wave of sheer frustration washed over me, making my jaw lock so hard I could feel the strain in my neck. I stared at the handwriting, each word a tiny, sharp barb. When my roommate finally noticed my rigid posture, they offered a weak, overly bright smile. "Just reading, Maya. Don't be so dramatic." The casualness of their tone scraped against my nerves. I snatched the book back, my breath coming in short, shallow gasps. I didn't speak; instead, I walked to my bed, dropping my backpack with a heavy thud that seemed disproportionately loud. I paced the length of the room three times before finally sitting down, my shoulders shaking with barely contained agitation, refusing to meet their eyes.

[story 3]

It was the sight of my meticulous notes, the vulnerability I’d poured onto those pages, that ignited a fierce heat beneath my skin. I felt a sudden, blinding surge of resentment. I moved with a sudden, coiled energy, crossing the distance to the desk in two strides. The air seemed to crackle between us. "Give that back," I stated, my voice dangerously level, every syllable measured and sharp. My hands hovered over the journal, trembling slightly, not from fear, but from the sheer force building inside me. My roommate finally lowered the volume of their reading, their expression shifting from detached to startled. They looked genuinely surprised by the sudden shift in my demeanor. I didn't wait for a reply; I simply snatched the book, the paper feeling hot beneath my fingertips, and retreated to my side of the room, breathing deeply, trying to force the internal turbulence back down into a manageable simmer.
